issue_title: [Bug] /funds/load returns 403 error.
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Upon loading of a fund by name or symbol the terminal returns:
```
openbb_terminal.mutual_funds.investpy_model|get_fund_historical|0|Exception: ERR#0015: error 403, try again later. 
- Traceback (most recent call last): 
- File cut/file/path/decorators.py, line 66, in wrapper - value = func(*args, **kwargs) 
- File cut/file/path/investpy_model.py, line 189, in get_fund_historical 
- investpy.funds.get_fund_historical_data( 
- File cut/file/path/funds.py, line 638, in get_fund_historical_data
- ConnectionError: ERR#0015: error 403, try again later.
```

**To Reproduce**
Open the terminal, go to funds, load a fund symbol.

**Screenshots**
-

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: MacOSX 11.6
- Python version 3.9.9

**Additional context**
According to the underlying usage of `investpy` the function `get_fund_historical_data` is broken due to an undocumented API deprecation by Investing.com. The analogue issue for stocks is tracked [here](https://github.com/alvarobartt/investpy/issues/600).
issue_comments:
As you mention, we are hoping that this gets patched up.   We can look into commenting out the function for the time being.

An interim fix could be  to load through the stocks menu.  For example, if I run `funds/overview`, I see FCNTX on there, which I can load using `stocks/load FCNTX`.  If you are looking outside the US, I am not sure if things will match up between yahoo's naming and investing.coms naming.
this error message is different, and is a recent development:

Hi, @jmaslek note that I released [`investiny`](https://github.com/alvarobartt/investiny) just a week ago and it supports more or less the same functionality as `investpy`, while I wait for Investing.com to answer my email to get to an agreement to continue developing `investpy`.

If you need help with the integration of `investiny` in your package let me know and I'll try to fill in a PR 🤗 All the best!

> this error message is different, and is a recent development: > This is just what is printed in the frontend. When you look at your logs under `~/OpenBBUserData/logs/` you will see the underlying log messages.

> Hi, @jmaslek note that I released [`investiny`](https://github.com/alvarobartt/investiny) just a week ago and it supports more or less the same functionality as `investpy`, while I wait for Investing.com to answer my email to get to an agreement to continue developing `investpy`.
> 
> If you need help with the integration of `investiny` in your package let me know and I'll try to fill in a PR 🤗 All the best!

Hi @alvarobartt!  Nice to chat with you :)

I did dig around slightly with the new package and was going to look into using that once you have the time stamps integrated (ummmm looks like you have added that now!). Currently I have only integrated your package with mutual fund menus but will be looking at adding more in the upcoming months.

If there are more areas you would like to integrate, we can chat :)

issue_title: [Bug] /Stocks/SIA - load function doesn't work. Error: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
issue_body: Recreate by entering the menu with no stock loaded, then load a ticker from any source.
issue_comments:
@deeleeramone I had a quick look at this and it looks like it's **intermittent**. The first time I loaded I got the same error (it makes no difference if it's loaded before going to `sia` or after).

The reason is that we load this data from Yahoo Finance and the data (which they call `summaryProfile` was missing altogether).

Making it not crash with that error is trivial, but my main question is how should we report this kind of errors? I am very new to this project, but it does look like that menu cannot be used unless we know the sector, country and industry.

A good solution would be to find an alternative source if we can't find it in Yahoo Finance, can someone help me understand if we have an alternative source implemented?

Hmmm, @catalintoma I think you are right. We are relying on the Sector, Industry and Country from Yahoo Finance. This is also because this menu utilizes FinanceDatabase which is built on top of Yahoo Finance.

So I think it is not trivial to use another source in here, unfortunately
Has this been fixed? I can't seem to reproduce it.

> Has this been fixed? I can't seem to reproduce it.

As I wrote in the first comment, it is **intermittent**
I was able to reproduce for 2-3 times, basically that quote endpoint from YF returns
`summaryProfile: {}` which gets deserialized to `None`, hence the error message.

I want to improve the error message and refactor some duplicate code in that controller tomorrow morning, but as @DidierRLopes said we don't have an alternative source, so not that much we can do.
Sounds good! I was just wanting to replace the NoneType error with a message that is more readable to a non coder.

issue_title: [Bug] Terminal settings are not being read from the file they are written
issue_body:
I noticed this because the contents of the old `.env` file in my OpenBBTerminal folder had been updated to be a blank file. Settings for plotautoscaling , dpi , and monitor are being read from the old location .env file. If there are no entries in this file, it goes to "default". 

You can test this by removing entries from the `.env` in the old location, changing a setting in the Terminal settings ( you will see this be updated in `./openbb_terminal/.env`) - like dpi 300 - restarting and then looking at the setting menu. You should see that any changes made will be forgotten.
issue_comments: I am not able to reproduce this. I changed DPI to the new value and then reloaded, and settings showed a DPI of 300. This issue has not been resolved. Another example is changing Timezone settings in the installer version. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/194399209-6caef42d-c416-444f-952d-665c20e98b67.png) I think the issue is that settings are not applied instantly. Once I closed and reopened the terminal I got the desired result. Let me dig into how to fix immediately.

issue_title: [Bug] Every graph throws Wayland/Qt errors constantly when open
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Every graph throws Wayland/Qt errors constantly when open which is:
> qt.qpa.wayland: Wayland does not support QWindow::requestActivate()

**To Reproduce**
1. Start using openbb in terminal
2. `crypto` command
3. `load btc` command
4. `candle` command
5. when graph opens, the errors constantly scroll by

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/549431/190465064-bc80f02b-b88c-489c-87a7-b69cbe9fb721.png)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Fedora Linux 36 (Workstation Edition)
- Python version: 3.10.6

**Additional context**
Add any other information that you think could be useful for us.

Wayland is the default window manager for Fedora Linux 36 and this looks like a compatiblity issue for wayland and Qt.  However, since it is being thrown in OpenBB, seems like a quick check on the DM to not make this call would fix this without too much friction.
issue_comments:
A comment here indicates this is expected so maybe it could just be avoided in the code.

https://forum.qt.io/topic/90639/comparing-qt-widgets-app-under-gnome-wayland-using-platform-wayland-egl-to-xcb/8

@ebdavison can you use tkinter in fedora instead of qt as a matplotlib backend?
in the terminal set `settings/backend` to `tkAgg` manually

Some context:
As of now the default matplotlib backend is set to None in the terminal settings. This leads to matplotlib performing a search for available backends and serving the "first" one available. Maybe there is some system path setting or other reason that Qt is offered before other backends.

I did try tkAgg and the result was the same.

However, I re-created by virtual environment with copies rather than symlinks and used 3.9.14 instead of 3.10.6 and now there are not more error messages.

So, issue seems to be solved by using Python 3.9.x rather than 3.10.x.

Seems like this should be in the documentation or in an FAQ and I checked and do not see it.  The installation docs don't talk about Linux at all either and this is most definitely possible despite the missing installer.

Happy to troubleshoot this more if needed.  If not, this issue can be closed.
I updated the docs to make it even more clear. Thanks for pointing this out!

issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Terminal gives incorrect changes over time when time is not daily.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72827203/187684496-bae54e95-70d1-49af-be05-a2d9fbe9a3e7.png)

issue_comments: @colin99d - it is even showing old data. Last price does not = last price. > @colin99d - it is even showing old data. Last price does not = last price. Took a look at this and it uses the previous days adjusted close. Thus all of the data on daily frequency is at least one day delayed. The problem with other time frequencies is that it just uses a fixed amount of data, for weekly return the 5 latest data points (with no adjustments).

issue_title: [Bug] cdebt (National Debt) function in economy menu incorrect/misleading
issue_body: **Description** cdebt function in the economy menu returns a table titled 'National Debt' with three columns (Country/Debt/Per Capita). Looking at the help function in the economy menu lists the source for this information as USDebtClock.org. This data didn't seem right to me so I had a look at the source code and it seems to be pulling from a table on wikipedia (not USDebtClock) which lists countries by **exernal** debt (not national/government debt). External debt is debt owed by the government, businesses and people of a country. External debt is still worth having but should be clearly labelled as such. **Screenshots** If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem. ----- **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: MacMonterey 12.5 - Python version 3.9
issue_comments: Thank you for catching this!! I messed this up because I could not figure out a way to scrape US Debt Clock. I will get on fixing this right away, also if you know of scraping the tables from US Debt Clock let me know and I will add the feature.

issue_title: [Bug]Portfolio/PO/Maxsharpe - Error: time-weighted interpolation only works on Series or DataFrames with a DatetimeIndex  Error: returns must be a DataFrame  Error: not enough values to unpack (expected 2, got 0)
issue_body:
```
2022 Aug 14, 18:28 /portfolio/po/ $ maxsharpe
Optimization can take time. Please be patient...

5 Failed downloads:
- FB: No data found, symbol may be delisted
- CERN: No data found, symbol may be delisted
- BF.B: No data found for this date range, symbol may be delisted
- BLL: No data found, symbol may be delisted
- BRK.B: No data found, symbol may be delisted
Error: time-weighted interpolation only works on Series or DataFrames with a DatetimeIndex

Error: returns must be a DataFrame

Error: not enough values to unpack (expected 2, got 0)
```
To recreate: `/portfolio/po/load -f SP_500_Portfolio.xlsx/maxsharpe`

The provided file, SP_500_Portfolio.xlsx uses an incompatible symbol formats for B-shares. `BRK.B` is the symbol when `--source polygon`, but when `--source yf` the symbol must be `BRK-B`

Ideally, there would be an automated way to retrieve all current symbols for the S&P 500 constituents and weightings without having to update and maintain this file manually, with it also being non-source-dependent for retrieving historical/meta data. 

A potential source for updated daily constituents data: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/andrewmvd/sp-500-stocks

Hi @soggyomelette can you take a look at this one ?
Thanks

issue_title: [IMPROVE] Index docs slightly out of date
issue_body:
Seems like the docs on the website have some minor differences with the help command, specifically --show vs --show_indicies or -si and -iv no longer works for interval. Minor but worth mentioning.

Help Command:
```
2022 Aug 08, 08:47 (🦋) /economy/ $ index --help
usage: index [-i INDICES [INDICES ...]] [--show] [--interval {1m,2m,5m,15m,30m,60m,90m,1h,1d,5d,1wk,1mo,3mo}] [-s START_DATE]
             [-e END_DATE] [-c COLUMN] [-q QUERY [QUERY ...]] [-r] [-h] [--export EXPORT] [--raw] [-l LIMIT]

Obtain any set of indices and plot them together. With the -si argument the major indices are shown. By using the arguments (for
example 'nasdaq' and 'sp500') you can collect data and plot the graphs together. [Source: Yahoo finance / FinanceDatabase]

optional arguments:
  -i INDICES [INDICES ...], --indices INDICES [INDICES ...]
                        One or multiple indices (default: None)
  --show                Show the major indices, their arguments and ticker (default: False)
  --interval {1m,2m,5m,15m,30m,60m,90m,1h,1d,5d,1wk,1mo,3mo}
                        The preferred interval data is shown at. This can be 1m, 2m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 60m, 90m, 1h, 1d, 5d, 1wk, 1mo or
                        3mo (default: 1d)
  -s START_DATE, --start START_DATE
                        The start date of the data (format: YEAR-MONTH-DAY, i.e. 2010-12-31) (default: 2000-01-01)
  -e END_DATE, --end END_DATE
                        The end date of the data (format: YEAR-MONTH-DAY, i.e. 2021-06-20) (default: None)
  -c COLUMN, --column COLUMN
                        The column you wish to load in, by default this is the Adjusted Close column (default: Adj Close)
  -q QUERY [QUERY ...], --query QUERY [QUERY ...]
                        Search for indices with given keyword (default: None)
  -r, --returns         Flag to show compounded returns over interval. (default: False)
  -h, --help            show this help message (default: False)
  --export EXPORT       Export raw data into csv, json, xlsx (default: )
  --raw                 Flag to display raw data (default: False)
  -l LIMIT, --limit LIMIT
                        Number of entries to show in data. (default: 10)

For more information and examples, use 'about index' to access the related guide.
```

Website docs:
```optional arguments:
  -i INDICES [INDICES ...], --indices INDICES [INDICES ...]
                        One or multiple indices (default: None)
  -si, --show_indices   Show the major indices, their arguments and ticker (default: False)
  -iv {1m,2m,5m,15m,30m,60m,90m,1h,1d,5d,1wk,1mo,3mo}, --interval {1m,2m,5m,15m,30m,60m,90m,1h,1d,5d,1wk,1mo,3mo}
                        The preferred interval data is shown at. This can be 1m, 2m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 60m, 90m, 1h, 1d, 5d, 1wk, 1mo or 3mo (default: 1d)
  -s START_DATE, --start_date START_DATE
                        The start date of the data (format: YEAR-MONTH-DAY, i.e. 2010-12-31) (default: 2000-01-01)
  -e END_DATE, --end_date END_DATE
                        The end date of the data (format: YEAR-MONTH-DAY, i.e. 2021-06-20) (default: None)
  -c COLUMN, --column COLUMN
                        The column you wish to load in, by default this is the Adjusted Close column (default: Adj Close)
  -q QUERY [QUERY ...], --query QUERY [QUERY ...]
                        Search for indices with given keyword (default: None)
  -st, --store          Store the data to be used for plotting with the 'plot' command. (default: False)
  -r, --returns         Flag to show compounded returns over interval. (default: False)
  -h, --help            show this help message (default: False)
  --export {csv,json,xlsx}
                        Export raw data into csv, json, xlsx (default: )
  --raw                 Flag to display raw data (default: False)
  -l LIMIT, --limit LIMIT
                        Number of entries to show in data. (default: 10)
```
issue_comments: Thanks for pointing this out!

issue_title: [Bug] When entering the comparison analysis menu with a loaded ticker containing a suffix, the suffix is not passed through to the `ticker` in the menu.
issue_body: When entering the comparison analysis menu with a loaded ticker containing a suffix, the suffix is not passed through to the `ticker` in the menu. The user must first `remove` and then `add` the primary ticker within the submenu prior to using the functions.

issue_title: [Bug] /economy/ecocal --category flag is broken. Argparse is not joining words.
issue_body: argparse is not joining words, and see categories as only the first word, resulting in an `invalid choice` ``` 2022 Aug 04, 13:48 /economy/ $ ecocal --categories economic activity -i medium usage: ecocal [-c COUNTRY [COUNTRY ...]] [-i {high,medium,low,all}] [-cat {employment,credit,balance,economic activity,central banks,bonds,inflation,confidence index} [{employment,credit,balance,economic activity,central banks,bonds,inflation,confidence index} ...]] [-s START_DATE] [-e END_DATE] [-h] [--export EXPORT] [--raw] [-l LIMIT] ecocal: error: argument -cat/--categories: invalid choice: 'economic' (choose from 'employment', 'credit', 'balance', 'economic activity', 'central banks', 'bonds', 'inflation', 'confidence index') 2022 Aug 04, 13:48 /economy/ $ ecocal --categories economic activity usage: ecocal [-c COUNTRY [COUNTRY ...]] [-i {high,medium,low,all}] [-cat {employment,credit,balance,economic activity,central banks,bonds,inflation,confidence index} [{employment,credit,balance,economic activity,central banks,bonds,inflation,confidence index} ...]] [-s START_DATE] [-e END_DATE] [-h] [--export EXPORT] [--raw] [-l LIMIT] ecocal: error: argument -cat/--categories: invalid choice: 'economic' (choose from 'employment', 'credit', 'balance', 'economic activity', 'central banks', 'bonds', 'inflation', 'confidence index') ``` @DidierRLopes has also mentioned elsewhere that functions with flags containing more than one letter should only use double-dashes because a single dash with more than one character can create errors. A solution would be to rename the flag as `--cat` and remove `--categories and -c`

issue_title: [Bug] Add in a test to check if logging into DEGIRO works
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
After settings my log-in details, trying to log-in within the `portfolio/bro/degiro` menu doesn't work. I don't have 2FA enabled on my DEGIRO account. See the following:

```
2022 Aug 02, 09:47 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ login
Error: 400 Client Error: Bad Request for url: https://trader.degiro.nl/login/secure/login

You are now logged in !
2022 Aug 02, 09:47 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ hold
Error: Connection required.

0 position found.
2022 Aug 02, 09:47 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $
```

I noticed that there are no tests that check if the log-in works: `defined, not tested`. **In the end the issue was that I set my _email_ instead of _username_ so the conclusion is that there needs to be a proper test and ideally, something that checks you don't input your email.**

**To Reproduce**
What I did:

1. Set my DEGIRO credentials within `keys` as follows `degiro -u EMAIL -p PASSWORD`
2. Try to log-in.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: [e.g. Mac Sierra]
- Python version [e.g. 3.6.8]

**Additional context**
Add any other information that you think could be useful for us.
issue_comments:
I am still unable to login to Degiro. I raised an issue with support and they updated the documentation for Degiro login which can be found here: https://openbb-finance.github.io/OpenBBTerminal/terminal/portfolio/bro/degiro/

Unfortunately, I am still unable to login. The `login -otp OneTimePassword` does not work for me. I am on Intel macOS using openBB v1.6. Maybe this feature was added in v1.7, but v1.7 is not yet available for Intel macOS.

I have updated to v1.8 of openBB and now the `login -otp` command is recognised. Unfortunately I am still unable to login.

```
2022 Sep 09, 16:43 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ login -otp 507104
Error: 'NoneType' object is not callable

You are now logged in !
2022 Sep 09, 16:43 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ hold
Error: 'NoneType' object is not callable

0 position found.
```
WIP, will add a PR very soon.

issue_title: [Bug] Installing new versions breaks when installing scipy 1.9.0
issue_body: **Describe the bug** Something in either the conda environment or numpy is causing `scipy` to update to 1.9.0. It currently breaks on M1 Macbooks since a dependency "Meson" needs OpenBlas to be linked, which isn't currently done correctly on M1/M2 architectures. **To Reproduce** 1. Checkout the `main` branch. 2. Run `poetry install`. It should fail when updating Scipy. **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: Mac M1 - Python 3.8.13 **Additional context** Current discussions for fixes are below in Scipy. https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/16308

issue_title: Bug] Crypto/OV/HM - limit of 50 returns Error: cannot convert float NaN to integer
issue_body:
When the limit touches 50, this error message displays.

```
 /crypto/ov/ $ hm stablecoins -l 50
Error: cannot convert float NaN to integer
```

When a `category` is selected, the % of market cap should then be calculated as relative to the % of the chosen category, and not the entire market cap of all cryptos. This is why everything is crowded into one tiny corner while the biggest pieces are fractions of one percent. 

![Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 8 04 59 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/182064202-39205617-4179-4b2f-b17a-3e4d15e9a19f.png)

![Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 8 06 41 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/182064352-9ee4ac57-4fdb-41c3-9e7d-a7a4814fab76.png)
issue_comments:
The help dialogue indicates this is ranked by market cap, but this is clearly not the % of BTC's market cap. There is nowhere to indicate what this value is, why it's there, and what it has to do with the % of total market cap.  

![Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 8 14 47 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/182065118-74771253-2d38-4f22-abdd-1778bf75c6a7.png)
@deeleeramone the % here represents the daily percentage change in the currency - I've updated the help for the hm command to say the same, hopefully that helps?

@soggyomelette One would eventually make that logical leap as a conclusion, but knowing the % change from today does little to help situations such as the screenshot above, the Top 20/40 Stable Coins. Ranking stable coins by a market cap value relative to the entire market makes all stable coins = 0% by the time you introduce rounding. This is why the large majority of coins are right on top of each other and is unreadable.

> @soggyomelette One would eventually make that logical leap as a conclusion, but knowing the % change from today does little to help situations such as the screenshot above, the Top 20/40 Stable Coins. Ranking stable coins by a market cap value relative to the entire market makes all stable coins = 0% by the time you introduce rounding. This is why the large majority of coins are right on top of each other and is unreadable.

It's just that looking at other heatmaps online (like this one https://www.tradingview.com/heatmap/stock/?color=change&dataset=SPX500&group=sector&size=market_cap_basic) I saw they used the percentage in the center to denote the same thing so I thought I would leave it unchanged. 

Also, looking at the code, it does in fact rank them relative to the category you choose but the market cap of some of the later stable coins is so minute that they get dwarfed by the much larger stable coins (https://www.coingecko.com/en/categories/stablecoins? for example check the market of $65B of USDT with even the 20th coin   with $108M - it's almost a 1000x less

I suppose the issue is in the clarity of the purpose for the function, and communicating that to the user. The text is also insanely small. Is there a way to auto-scale the size of the text?

![Screen Shot 2022-08-07 at 4 08 08 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/183314599-66c41d89-2b03-43a8-8f23-123aaefada67.png)

issue_title: [Bug] Options/Chains - yFinance Source creates an error where --source Tradier does not. Error: float division by zero. Error: 'list' object has no attribute 'fillna'
issue_body: To reproduce, load AMZN, enter options, select the expiration for 2022-12-16, enter `chains` ``` /stocks/options/ $ chains Error: float division by zero Error: 'list' object has no attribute 'fillna' ``` A different expiration date does not produce the error, but Tradier as a source has no problems with it. A couple of notes on this command as well: - Why does the function not respect `load [ticker] --source user_choice`? - The table has a different title when Tradier is the source. Are Greeks being calculated in a different way here?
issue_comments: This is a result of yahoo finance giving some wacky chains. If I look at this AMZN chain, there are some wild values (probably tracking pre split -- note the 2300 strike in the below), and these have IV = 0. See below. Since for yahoo, we calculate the greeks on our own (which have values \propto 1/IV, this gives the "Divide by zero") > Why does the function not respect load [ticker] --source user_choice? You mean the menu? Not sure. Should just wrap the same load function as the stocks controller. > The table has a different title when Tradier is the source. Are Greeks being calculated in a different way here? Yeah I rewrote this yahoo function, including the table recently and didn't touch any tradier functions. Note that the greeks just get pulled from tradier, so its not the same calculation (in theory should be comparable if they use a BS model). Since tradier is not available to all new members, its lifespan is likely not too long here. The TODOs for a fix on this would be to clean up the options chains and get rid of anything with an IV = 0

RE: The split, that is definitely what it is. The contracts are still outstanding, but they are not trading. An easy filter for would be based on the # of 0s in the strike's symbol ID string. 

[ticker]+[yymmdd][c or p][xxxxxxxx] where x is 0-9 and the last two digits are the 1/100th of a dollar. So a filter would sweep [00xxxxxx] where the contracts with two zeroes before a digit are the keepers. Of course, this wouldn't work if the stock was $500, or was recently. 

![Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 6 18 55 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/182054846-d52f8ddf-9380-42fe-abf1-1e88b7dd4e3c.png)

RE: The load function - `load --source` currently only has any meaning on the expiration dates, but it should be taking the entire data set from the source to pass it through the standardized calculations that you have built for the greeks [including the second order that are from `greeks -a`]

![Screen Shot 2022-07-31 at 6 35 05 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/182056040-f0b36c98-7c66-4161-9d9f-e6200b9a2de1.png)

The table for `greeks -a` and `chains --source yf` is now limiting the number of decimals that are displayed, which makes all greeks look like 0. What was previously the case was that `chains` would adapt the size of the table to the width of the screen, and show [0.0000...] dots to indicate that the numbers were not being rounded, just limited by space. **This is very important because you cannot round these values, or have an arbitrary point at which the number of zeros gets chopped off.**

issue_title: [IMPROVE] (economy) Add economy/ycrv into the python package API to allow for jupyter interaction.
issue_body:
**What's the feature that should be improved?**

Add ycrv to the python API. We had a call to suggest a possible fix highlighted below. The issue is this immediately launches a display view, but it probably needs to be broken down to return a Pandas Series.

**Describe how you would like the feature improved**

This is a fix where it immediately launches a plot.

In `economy_api.py` add lines:

```python
from .fred_view import display_yield_curve as ycrv
from .investingcom_view import display_yieldcurve as ycrv2
```

Ideally, it should return a Series then allow the user to plot, or add to a dataframe.

**Possibly describe the ideal way to improve this**
- ycrv 2 is for yield curve countries that aren't the US. Maybe a combined function just called `ycrv` wrapping both would be nicer.

**Additional information**
- Probably should go into #2097

issue_title: [Bug] Login Issues with Degiro Broker integration
issue_body:
### Issue

When attempting to use the Degiro integration while having provided succesfully my user name, password and otp secret, login fails with this output and no other actions are allowed.

```bash
2022 Jul 21, 13:16 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ login
Error: 'NoneType' object is not callable

You are now logged in !

2022 Jul 21, 13:16 (🦋) /portfolio/bro/degiro/ $ topnews
Error: 'NoneType' object is not callable

Error: 'list' object has no attribute 'DESCRIPTOR'
```

### To reproduce
```bash
export OPENBB_DG_USERNAME={} OPENBB_DG_PASSWORD={} OPENBB_DG_TOTP_SECRET={}
/Applications/OpenBB\ Terminal/OpenBB\ Terminal
portfolio/bro/degiro
login
```
- OS: macOS Monterrey
- Python version 3.6.8

### Additional
I've tried using directly the `degiro_connector` library (the one your integration is using) and it was possible to login providing the same credentials. IMO we can discard that my account has a specific issue that makes the library fail.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1226151/180202501-84cdedc6-fe2f-4503-8768-3f60b276538c.png)
issue_comments: Any updates on this? Anything I can do for helping?

Hi @perezpaya, apparently you were not logged in.

There were some issues solved by this PR https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/pull/2234. We also updated login instructions here https://openbb-finance.github.io/OpenBBTerminal/terminal/portfolio/bro/degiro/.

TLDR:

Go to `keys` and type credentials `degiro -u USERNAME -p PASSWORD`
Go to `/portfolio/bro/degiro` and type `login` if no 2FA
Go to `/portfolio/bro/degiro` and type `login -otp ONETIMEPASSWORD` if 2FA enabled

Can you check if you still have problems?

issue_title: [Bug] Reports Menu Not Loading
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When I try load the Reports Menu, I get the following error: "Error: list index out of range"

**To Reproduce**

- Install OpenBBTerminal using the Python Install Instructions; make sure to use the full env file.
- Type conda activate gst
- pip install requirements-full.txt
- python terminal.py
- reports

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Win10
- Python version: 3.8.13

**Additional context**
This occurred after recently upgrading.
issue_comments: I cannot recreate this 😢 Do you have the latest version? Can you check your last commit by doing: `git log` ?
I would try `poetry install` as well because some dependencies do not get installed by `pip install -r requirements.txt` and instead rely on Poetry.

@deeleeramone - Thank you for the suggestion. I tried Poetry & got a missing dep error.

`(base) conda activate gst`

`(gst) poetry install`
`The virtual environment found in anaconda3\envs\gst seems to be broken.
Recreating virtualenv openbbterminal-6AnzOeX4-py3.8 in AppData\Local\pypoetry\Cache\virtualenvs\openbbterminal-6AnzOeX4-py3.8
Installing dependencies from lock file`

`Package operations: 303 installs, 0 updates, 0 removals`

`(gst) python terminal.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "terminal.py", line 14, in <module>
    import dotenv
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'dotenv'`

Checking the Poetry log does show:
  • Installing python-dotenv (0.19.2)
  
@DidierRLopes - Thank you for checking. I have some custom reports; is it possible that's causing the error under pip install?

I removed my custom reports from the directory and was able to get it to load using the pip install.

Root cause appears to be that my reports used the old underscore based naming convention; like due_diligence.ipynb. Renamed it to dd.ipynb & the menu loaded again.

It looks like Poetry just attempted to build the whole environment without Conda as the name of the virtual environment in your path looks suspiciously like when that happens. It appears that you are interacting with a different virtual environment handler now, `virtualenv`, instead of Anaconda.

When stuff like this starts happening on my local installation, I find that it's just easier to delete the environment and rebuild it from with the `--file /build/conda/conda-3-8-env.yaml` step, followed by activating the new environment just built, and then running `poetry install`

issue_title: Open BB terminal fails to start on Mac M1
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Open BB terminal fails to start on Apple M1.

**To Reproduce**
Followed the steps at:
https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/blob/main/openbb_terminal/README.md#installing-the-terminal

**Command Line logs**
python terminal.py
Intel MKL FATAL ERROR: This system does not meet the minimum requirements for use of the Intel(R) Math Kernel Library.
The processor must support the Intel(R) Supplemental Streaming SIMD Extensions 3 (Intel(R) SSSE3) instructions.
The processor must support the Intel(R) Streaming SIMD Extensions 4.2 (Intel(R) SSE4.2) instructions.
The processor must support the Intel(R) Advanced Vector Extensions (Intel(R) AVX) instructions.


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Mac Monterey
- Python version 3.8.3

**Additional context**
Add any other information that you think could be useful for us.
issue_comments:
Hello, it appears that the wrong version of Numpy/Scipy has been installed in your virtual environment. For Mac M1, I would recommend Miniforge: https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge, and not Anaconda. Then,

- `conda install git`
-  clone the repo
-  build the environment, then run:
- `poetry install`
- `conda install -c tensorflow==2.8.1`
- try launching again.

The `Intel MKL FATAL ERROR:` usually appears when tensorflow is not installed from `conda-forge` channel. That's the only place they have binaries that work for both intel and apple silicon.

There were some updates in the dependency tree last week that will enable us having native m1 support soon. In the meant time download the latest DMG for macs from the releases https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/releases

issue_title: [Bug] Economy/treasury: No data found for any secondary market treasuries. Displays a blank chart.
issue_body:
This data exists in the FRED catalogue, but is not returned through the Treasury function. All combinations of secondary market rates produce the same error. 

```
(🦋) /economy/ $ treasury -m 3m -f daily -t secondary
No data found for the combination secondary and 3m.
```
```
(🦋) /economy/ $ treasury -m 3m -f weekly -t secondary
No data found for the combination secondary and 3m.
```

```
(🦋) /economy/ $ treasury -m 3m -t secondary
No data found for the combination secondary and 3m.
```

From Fred: 

Daily: 
![Screen Shot 2022-06-14 at 9 50 46 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/173633710-52c03d48-b39d-4e06-8094-5503a2081f64.png)

Weekly:
![Screen Shot 2022-06-14 at 9 56 51 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/173633876-106ac844-7ab6-42d3-ab07-32a6f42b5d90.png)

3 month secondary market:
![Screen Shot 2022-06-14 at 9 58 45 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/173634284-bde21d56-120b-47c8-9521-4234ef6e576f.png)

To fix, the links need to be made to the correct series IDs (in order of monthly, weekly, daily):

```
4 week series IDs: TB4WK, WTB4WK, DTB4WK

3 month series IDs: TB3MS, WTB3MS, DTB3

6 month series IDS: TB6MS, WTB6MS, DTB6

1 year series IDS: TB1YR, WTB1YR, DTB1YR

```

issue_title: [Bug] Pressing ^d at the command prompt causes an Exception
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
^d does not end the terminal nicely

**To Reproduce**
Press ctrl-D at the terminal prompt


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Debian 11
- Python version 3.8.13

**Additional context**
This patch will fix it:

```
diff --git a/terminal.py b/terminal.py
index a63522cf8..b07ee75dc 100644
--- a/terminal.py
+++ b/terminal.py
@@ -489,7 +489,7 @@ def terminal(jobs_cmds: List[str] = None, appName: str = "gst"):
                             completer=t_controller.completer,
                             search_ignore_case=True,
                         )
-                except KeyboardInterrupt:
+                except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
                     print_goodbye()
                     break
             # Get input from user without auto-completion
```

It's such a small change, I'm not too worried
Sounds good!

issue_title: [Bug] stocks/dps/FTD - Start/End flags are broken: 404 error & Error: list indices must be integers or slices, not str
issue_body:
![Screen Shot 2022-06-05 at 1 28 54 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/172069400-c64c316a-0c43-4a91-9461-816da795562f.png)

using `n -500` works, but specifying date windows does not. 

![Screen Shot 2022-06-05 at 1 33 51 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/172069462-db979640-3dcb-4964-b203-2ece0302a868.png)
issue_comments:
This bug results from a wrong link resulting in an HTTP 404 error. When looking on the SEC website at the .zip links (https://www.sec.gov/data/foiadocsfailsdatahtm) occasionally a .zip path will not be structured in the same way that a majority of the other paths are. 
Where the standard path is:
`...data/fails-to-deliver/cnsfails...`
There are some that are structured as: 
`...data/frequently-requested-foia-document-fails-to-deliver/cnsfails...` 
or 
`node/add/data_distribution/cnsfails...`
etc

The location of non-standard paths is fairly random. I suggest refactoring and utilizing the above link to scrape a list of the paths as to not run into this error.

Per Darren, we could potentially switch to FINRA API however this is only came online recently, so there has been no development on implementing this API as a source. As a result we will simply patch this bug and look into utilizing the API for 'fails to deliver' functionality.

issue_title: [Bug] Portfolio - Efficient Frontier has lost the Asset Labels
issue_body:
![Screen Shot 2022-05-05 at 1 26 28 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/166887274-3c721a26-0252-4c0f-a248-cd70fa931012.png)

what it used to look like:


![](https://res.cloudinary.com/practicaldev/image/fetch/s--RUWwZ4cL--/c_limit%2Cf_auto%2Cfl_progressive%2Cq_auto%2Cw_880/https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/nnj8qarpxo80yrn360hn.png)
issue_comments: @deeleeramone  how could I replicate that?

This is the portfolio file used for the unlabeled chart:


[BuyList.csv](https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/files/8636261/BuyList.csv)

![Screen Shot 2022-05-05 at 2 54 00 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/167031860-f6ea36d9-687e-433a-b482-605ab554c431.png)

To print it, load the portfolio and then:
```
po
ef
```

The labels disappeared once before, after a global change, and @jmaslek patched it up.

Not so sure if you would want these labels in the first place. You are rarely optimizing a portfolio with just a few tickers as it makes little sense especially with the current method. Maximizing for Sharpe for example just gives you a very concentrated portfolio which is exactly what you do not want. If I would input 50-100+ tickers, you would completely clutter the graph. 

I therefore think it is good the way it is, it gives a sense of direction where your portfolio lies on the efficient frontier but, being so dependent on set parameters and the mean-variance method used being rather sub-optimal, does not have tremendous value in the first place.
What if we only showed labels if there was below a certain amount of tickers? @JerBouma

I don't necessarily agree with this because without any labels you have no idea which assets are attributable to the overall calculations. How would one be able to adjust for weights? This is also where an interactive chart using mouse-activated labels becomes very useful.

https://linuxize.com/post/how-to-install-gcc-on-ubuntu-20-04/

You need to install GCC compiler on your system

export PYTHONPATH='local path/miniconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/'

Updating the git and pip packages can help, and you can also install Python 3.8.13.
```
conda update git 
conda update pip
conda install -c conda-forge python==3.8.13
```

issue_title: [Bug] Reddit API Keys: Only 4/5 Inputs Are Sent To .env
issue_body:
[Bug] Reddit API Keys: Only 4/5 Inputs Are Sent To .env

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85772166/161874127-4f0f3ce4-6832-411c-b6af-c5685713b590.png)

This results in the wrong fields being populated by input. For example,
```
OPENBB_API_REDDIT_PASSWORD='Danglewood69420'
```
This is my username. To remedy this, I edited the .env file to add this line: 

```
OPENBB_API_REDDIT_USERNAME='Danglewood69420'

issue_title: pandas-market-calendars required but not in requirements.txt
issue_body:
pandas-market-calendars is required to run terminal.py
It is in requirements-full.txt but not in requirements.txt

I just used pip install -r requirements.txt in Linux instead of using anaconda
issue_comments:
Hey @hurricane-dorian 

Have you tried using `poetry` for dependency management? We've adopted poetry for this project and everyone seems to like it.

In the project root in your venv (or other python environment) you can do:

```bash
pip install poetry
poetry install
```
and poetry will install all the requirements from the .lock file.

The requirements.txt that are in the repo might be lagging behind poetry.lock a bit
In poetry.lock "pandas-market-calendars" is marked as a dev package but it is required to run

> In poetry.lock "pandas-market-calendars" is marked as a dev package but it is required to run

That's a good catch! Thanks a lot. This issue will be closed once the PR is merged

issue_title: [Bug] Cannot load Coinbase portfolio
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When trying to load a Coinbase portfolio an error occurs. The error indicates an authentication issue, but it is not. The error happens when attempting to get a price on an invalid pair (USD-USD or XRP-USD) The response array contains all the currencies in a portfolio. The code tries to get a price for <CRYPTO>-<CURRENCY>

**: CoinbaseApiException: Invalid Authentication: {"message":"NotFound"}**

**Error: 'list' object has no attribute 'empty'**


**To Reproduce**
NOTE: This requires that you set up your Coinbase Pro API key and secret
At the terminal:
portfolio
bro
cb
account


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Windows wsl2
- Python version 3.8

**Additional context**
Error occurs on coinbase_model.py:79 When to_get == "USD-USD"
`make_coinbase_request(f"/products/{to_get}/stats", auth=auth)`

To validate this, I tested with the following changes and I was able to load my portfolio
1. coinbase_model.py:75
`    for index, row in df.iterrows():
`
2. Add after coinbase_model.py:76
`
            if to_get in { "USD-USD", "XRP-USD" }:
                df.drop(index,inplace=True)
                continue
`

The drop is needed otherwise this line fails coinbase_model.py:85
`        df["current_price"] = current_prices
`
issue_comments: @marcelonyc Nice catch! Do you want to create a PR for this and we'll review and get it merged? :D
@minhhoang1023 I will work on a fix a create a PR later today.

issue_title: [Bug] slash issue
issue_body: **Describe the bug** A "bad" load from the econometrics menu will crash the terminal. "Bad" loads have included so far: not giving an alias along with the filename, and setting the filename directly without using the auto-complete feature (which is dope, ty.) **To Reproduce** from the terminal, enter the econometrics menu and try to explicitly load a filepath (i used one i just stored from FRED data) - `load -f /home//code/OpenBBTerminal/exports/icsa-test.csv` with or without an `alias` it fails, `load: error: argument -f/--file: expected at least one argument` which it not accurate, as it was given an argument. **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: (Host) Mac OS X 12.2.1 running Parallels running (Guest) Ubuntu 20.04.2 ARM64 - Python version = 3.8.13 **Additional context** Should be easy to reproduce, hmu in Discord otherwise, happy to help troubleshoot.
issue_comments:
@andr0id3000 Looks like an issue here:
https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/blob/5cc2ee9e142837b5d92750c5bd43a82d1419e1c8/openbb_terminal/parent_classes.py#L160-L171

If it finds the `/` character anywhere in your input it splits your command into seperate commands by `/` (if I explained it properly)

For example, your command (`load -f /home/q/code/OpenBBTerminal/exports/icsa-test.csv`) gets changed to:
```sh
$ load -f
gives the error
$ home
changes to home
$ q
quit the program
```
I don't know why it does this, but we should probably change the character used to something like `;` so we can enter paths.

You could go into the afformentioned piece of code and change:
https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/blob/5cc2ee9e142837b5d92750c5bd43a82d1419e1c8/openbb_terminal/parent_classes.py#L161

to
```py
        elif ";" in an_input:
```
and
https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal/blob/5cc2ee9e142837b5d92750c5bd43a82d1419e1c8/openbb_terminal/parent_classes.py#L162

to
```py
            actions = an_input.split(";")
```
I'll open a PR if this fixes it for you.

For right now the correct way to do this would be to send arguments with a `/` character in them through quotation marks like this: `load -f :/home/<user>/code/OpenBBTerminal/exports/icsa-test.csv"`. However, I understand this is unintuitive and will get with the team on the best resolution of this. Thanks for the great explanation @bolshoytoster.

This is exactly right. This is something related with #1599 and it's down to the way we allow the user to create a queue of commands. 

This is also how we are able to run routines. We rely strongly on the "slash" separator, thus when a command requires it, this issue occurs. 

Examples are `exe` or `star history` on the new open source menu. The way we have been doing it is consuming the next command from the queue and updating the queue, which is very hacky and definitely a temporary solution.

I will have a go at a solution for this tonight. I will try to basically create a flag where in certain commands the user will not be able to use the slash operator. This is the only think I can think since it's impossible to allow for both as you wouldn't know when user wanted to use slash for another command or for a path.
@DidierRLopes you could instead use a different character, I suggested `;` because it's commonly used in things like bash already.

Is there a way to check if the command is an argument if the path does not exist, or to check throw up a warning that says "if there is a `/` in the comments use quotations. Also, could we look into having maybe using `\` and `/` differently to create a fix (or would this add more confusion).

issue_title: [Bug] Unable to load data for some cryptocurrencies (LTC-USD@Coinbase for example)
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
When I try to load data for LTC-USD from Coinbase I get an error

**Error: 'float' object has no attribute 'upper'**

**To Reproduce**
```
/crypto 
load --coin ltc --source cb --vs USD  -i 1day

```
**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: Windows wsl2
- Python version 3.8

**Additional context**
I traced the issue to how the crypto maps get loaded and how the Crypto name is retrieved. It seems that, in the case of LTC, some entries from the Yahofinance map add too many **nan** rows. I tried to figure out a way to load this, but I am having some trouble understanding how / why the crypto maps are loaded.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12512887/161404519-b06b2560-9df9-4dce-afd3-a5bc0f31c070.png)

If I change this line, I can load LTC-USD

cryptocurrency_helpers.py: 347
`    coins_map_df = prepare_all_coins_df().set_index("Symbol").dropna()
`

The error occurs when you get to this line 
cryptocurrency_helpers.py:927
`        coin, currency = symbol_coinbase.upper(), currency.upper()
`
symbol_coinbase is empty because this code line returns nan
cryptocurrency_helpers.py:487

`                coin_map_df.iloc[0]
`

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12512887/161404607-2f00cebd-1d58-430b-8d44-9f9c09531d56.png)

**Proposed solution**
If I understand the map loading process, the goal is to get a list of the coins available on each exchange and link them to other exchanges by the coin symbol. I believe the problem is that at least one list from Yahoo Finance, has the same Symbol for many pairs. This will cause duplicate records and later break the symbol lookup but return **nan** when a value actually exists.

I propose the following
* When loading each map, drop duplicates
     For example: `yahoo_finance_map.drop_duplicates(subset = "Symbol, inplace = True)`

* Drop na before iloc[0] when doing a symbol lookup for an exchange
      For example: coin_map_df.dropna().iloc[0]
issue_comments:
@marcelonyc I believe it's just a type mismatch (calling `upper` on a float).

Could you send a link to a dataset that causes this error?
@bolshoytoster I am loading from Coinbase directly. The error happens before the data is loaded. It works for btc or eth, but not for ltc.

@marcelonyc Looks like this occurs when a `openbb_terminal/cryptocurrency/data/*_gecko_map.json` file is missing the specified currency.

The best solution is probably to either add a warning and/or use a default value.

I've implemented a warning:
```diff
diff --git a/openbb_terminal/cryptocurrency/cryptocurrency_helpers.py b/openbb_terminal/cryptocurrency/cryptocurrency_helpers.py
index b6b6ab1..ed870fa 100644
--- a/openbb_terminal/cryptocurrency/cryptocurrency_helpers.py
+++ b/openbb_terminal/cryptocurrency/cryptocurrency_helpers.py
@@ -832,6 +832,16 @@ def load_ta_data(
         quoted currency
     """
 
+    if pd.isna(coin_map_df[{
+        "bin": "Binance",
+        "cp": "CoinPaprika",
+        "cg": "CoinGecko",
+        "cb": "Coinbase",
+        "yf": "YahooFinance",
+    }[source]]):
+        console.print(f"{source} doesn't support the specified --coin")
+        return pd.DataFrame(), ""
+
     limit = kwargs.get("limit", 100)
     interval = kwargs.get("interval", "1day")
     days = kwargs.get("days", 30)
```
I can open a PR if you want.
@bolshoytoster  Your change does not fix the problem. LTC-USD is a valid Coinbase pair.

issue_body: /jupyter/dashboards - failure to launch. I know I have Voila installed because I have been using it already, this problem is new. Ran git pull & poetry install just now. ``` (gst) Danglewoods-MacBook-Pro:gamestonkterminal danglewood$ git pull remote: Enumerating objects: 9, done. remote: Counting objects: 100% (9/9), done. remote: Compressing objects: 100% (1/1), done. remote: Total 5 (delta 4), reused 5 (delta 4), pack-reused 0 Unpacking objects: 100% (5/5), 507 bytes | 56.00 KiB/s, done. From https://github.com/GamestonkTerminal/GamestonkTerminal d00b098b9..6ee2a001a feat-add-yf-crypto -> origin/feat-add-yf-crypto Already up to date. (gst) Danglewoods-MacBook-Pro:gamestonkterminal danglewood$ poetry install Installing dependencies from lock file Package operations: 0 installs, 2 updates, 0 removals - Updating pytz (2021.3 -> 2022.1) - Updating grpcio (1.42.0 -> 1.45.0) Installing the current project: GamestonkTerminal (0.1.0) (gst) Danglewoods-MacBook-Pro:gamestonkterminal danglewood$ python terminal.py ```
issue_comments: Looks like Voila [does not work with the newest version of Jinja](https://github.com/voila-dashboards/voila/pull/1127). The PR shown above is still failing tests, so it might be some time before it is merged. I think the best strategy for now is to force poetry to use a lower version of Jinja. However, we should keep this bug open so we remember to take out the forced lower version of Jinja later. Want thoughts from @DidierRLopes or @piiq before I do this though.
I've previously pinned jinga version to 3.0.3 to make it work. I'll address it again in the PyInstaller PR 'cause I will be touching dep files there
Sounds good to me!

Regarding load commands i think the whole command needs to be updated to use [ccxt package](https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt#usage). Since the loading from source to source differs a lot it's difficult to tackle all these different situations where the ticker can be btc or bitcoin and then some sources compare it against different stablecoins (usdt, busd, usdc,...) and others compare directly against fiat like kraken (bitcoin-eur, bitcoin-usd)

True!

However, in this case the first screenshot corresponds to a missing "return", so even if we were using ccxt and the ticker wasn't found there would still be an issue

I'm fixing this bug! The first one is already fixed.

For the second screenshot, @DidierRLopes @jose-donato from coingecko, we don't have Volume data and hence the KeyError. Did we have this volume data previously?
@minhhoang1023 i think it was possible before. coinpaprika (--source cp) provides volume if you wanna set that one as default temporarily

issue_title: [Bug] Stocks/Candle: Moving Average flag has defaulted to on, and it can't be turned off.
issue_body:

issue_title: [Bug] /stocks/disc/pipo - the limit flag does not work as anticipated. default is 5, but 12 results returned; more than 10 is returned when stated limit is 10.
issue_comments: @deeleeramone The limit here refers to the limit of past days to look for IPOs, not the number of returned results. I understand that we typical use limit referred to # of returned results in other commands, that might be confusing here.

Ah. I understand when you explain like that. My ape brain has just been going through updating documentation, and have been replacing all “-n” labels with the updated “-l”. We should try to follow convention wherever possible, so perhaps we can include both? We could utilize either of the flags -d (number of days for look-back); or, -s (start date yyyy-mm-dd). What is the historical window available to view?

> 
> 
> @deeleeramone <https://github.com/deeleeramone> The limit here refers to the limit of past days to look for IPOs, not the number of returned results. I understand that we typical use limit referred to # of returned results in other commands, that might be confusing here.

We could add the number of limits though by default IMO one should be turned off. As otherwise, we'd basically cut off some IPOs arbitrary (e.g. there are 11 IPOs in the past 5 days but only 10 selected)

So we can specify as far in the past as you would like

My personal preference is to show as much as practically possible by default, unless it causes problems or just populates too much screen with 100s of lines. issue_title: [Bug] Wrong API key name for FRED on README.md
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
On `README.md` under the "API Keys" table, the variable name for the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) is incorrect.  The README lists `GT_FRED_API_KEY` but it needs to be `GT_API_FRED_KEY` as seen in `gamestonk_terminal/config_terminal.py`

**To Reproduce**
1. Go to https://github.com/GamestonkTerminal/GamestonkTerminal/
2. Take a look at the table "API Keys" https://github.com/GamestonkTerminal/GamestonkTerminal/#api-keys

**Additional context**
I was wondering why I kept on getting "Bad Request.  The value for variable api_key is not a 32 character alpha-numeric lower-case string.  Read https://research.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/api_key.html for more information. " error when trying to get any data under `econ` menu.  I know my API key is correct and the format of my `.env` file.  I took a look at the `gamestonk_terminal/config_terminal.py` file and sure enough, the variable name for the FRED API did not match up.
issue_comments: Thanks for the catch!  I am updating this now

issue_title: [Bug] Crashes on tkinter.TclError
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Attempting to run a command that will launch an external GUI will crash instead of erroring gracefully if tkinter is unable to connect to a display.

**To Reproduce**
1. Run these commands from CLI:
- `python terminal.py`
- `load GME`
- `view`
2. Crashes
(see full console at end)

**Screenshots**
N/A

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Windows 10/WSL2
 - Python version: 3.8.2

**Additional context**
I'm trying to get an X server working on Windows (new environment) and in doing so encountered this error.

Full console output:

```(gst) david@DESKTOP-EGUQI77:~/GamestonkTerminal$ python terminal.py

Welcome to Gamestonk Terminal 🚀

What do you want to do?
   help        help to see this menu again
   quit        to abandon the program

   clear       clear a specific stock ticker from analysis
   load        load a specific stock ticker for analysis
   candle      view a candle chart for a specific stock ticker
   view        view and load a specific stock ticker for technical analysis

Stock: ?
Market CLOSED.

Menus:
   disc        discover trending stocks,         e.g. map, sectors, high short interest
   mill        papermill menu,                   menu to generate notebook reports
   sen         sentiment of the market,          from: reddit, stocktwits, twitter
   fred        economic data,                    from: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis

(✨)> load GME
Loading Daily GME stock for analysis.

(✨)> view
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "terminal.py", line 255, in <module>
    main()
  File "terminal.py", line 149, in main
    view(l_args, s_ticker, s_start, s_interval, df_stock)
  File "/home/david/GamestonkTerminal/gamestonk_terminal/main_helper.py", line 404, in view
    plot_view_stock(df_stock.iloc[:, ln_col_idx], ns_parser.s_ticker)
  File "/home/david/GamestonkTerminal/gamestonk_terminal/helper_funcs.py", line 44, in plot_view_stock
    _, axVolume = plt.subplots(figsize=plot_autoscale(), dpi=cfgPlot.PLOT_DPI)
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/cbook/deprecation.py", line 451, in wrapper
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/pyplot.py", line 1287, in subplots
    fig = figure(**fig_kw)
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/pyplot.py", line 687, in figure
    figManager = new_figure_manager(num, figsize=figsize,
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/pyplot.py", line 315, in new_figure_manager
    return _backend_mod.new_figure_manager(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/backend_bases.py", line 3494, in new_figure_manager
    return cls.new_figure_manager_given_figure(num, fig)
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/site-packages/matplotlib/backends/_backend_tk.py", line 885, in new_figure_manager_given_figure
    window = tk.Tk(className="matplotlib")
  File "/home/david/anaconda3/envs/gst/lib/python3.8/tkinter/__init__.py", line 2270, in __init__
    self.tk = _tkinter.create(screenName, baseName, className, interactive, wantobjects, useTk, sync, use)
_tkinter.TclError: couldn't connect to display "192.168.0.1:0"
(gst) david@DESKTOP-EGUQI77:~/GamestonkTerminal$```
issue_comments:
When you run view, the program opens a window. Your WSL2 is not connected to your Windows UI, so the window fails to open.

This behavior is expected.

On Windows, you want to run the terminal outside of WSL/WSL2.

If you really really desire to run the terminal in WSL, you need to start an X server on your windows and do a correct DISPLAY export in your WSL shell.

I don't doubt that the behavior is expected, I'm raising it as a bug because the user experience is poor. Rather than crashing out in this instance it would be preferred to have a message like "Could not connect to display. Check your settings." and continue operating.
That is true.

issue_title: Using wrong API key for AV earnings data
issue_body:
This call should be `&apikey={cfg.API_KEY_ALPHAVANTAGE}`. It seems like their api auth sucks right now and it will happily accept `&apikey=myfakeapikey` but this should probably be fixed. [According to their docs](https://www.alphavantage.co/documentation/), this is a required parameter.

https://github.com/DidierRLopes/GamestonkTerminal/blob/bdaaca453322e1be8687040e26f2b984beeb9855/gamestonk_terminal/fundamental_analysis/alpha_vantage_api.py#L378-L381

Thank you for the report!

Here's the fix - https://github.com/DidierRLopes/GamestonkTerminal/pull/204

issue_title: `popular`command unexpected results
issue_body:
At the moment if find stocks only in `wallstreetbets` subreddit. 

```
> popular
Search for latest tickers under 50 'pennystocks' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'RobinHoodPennyStocks' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'Daytrading' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'StockMarket' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'stocks' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'investing' posts
  0 tickers found.
Search for latest tickers under 50 'wallstreetbets' posts
  23 tickers found.
```
issue_comments: Same!

Hey guys,

This is unexpected, what tickers were you using?

The only thing I can think of is that this command looks for 50 posts under a sub, from the past day, but one of the conditions that the code has is to not consider posts that have been removed by the mods. What could have happened is that it looks through 50 posts that have just been removed by the mods. Although it seems unlikely.

Can you try to use: `-l 500` which defines the limit of posts to search to 500. (I believe this is the maximum allowed by  `search_submissions`function.

Let me know how it goes

> Hey guys,
> 
> This is unexpected, what tickers were you using?

What could have happened is that it looks through 50 posts that have just been removed by the mods. Although it seems unlikely.

> Can you try to use: `-l 500` which defines the limit of posts to search to 500. (I believe this is the maximum allowed by `search_submissions`function.
>
